en: After this there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
zh: 這些事以後，到了猶太人的一個節期，耶穌就上耶路撒冷去。

en: Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool, in Aramaic called Bethesda, which has five roofed colonnades.
zh: 在耶路撒冷靠近羊門有一個水池，希伯來話叫作畢士大，池邊有五條走廊。

en: In these lay a multitude of invalids- blind, lame, and paralyzed.
zh: 在那裡躺著許多病人，有瞎眼的、瘸腿的和癱瘓的。

en: One man was there who had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
zh: 那裡有一個人，病了三十八年。

en: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had already been there a long time, he said to him, "Do you want to be healed?"
zh: 耶穌看見他躺著，知道他病了很久，就問他：“你要痊愈嗎？”

en: The sick man answered him, "Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, and while I am going another steps down before me."
zh: 病人回答：“先生，水動的時候，沒有人把我放在池裡；自己想去的時候，總是給別人搶先。”

en: Jesus said to him, "Get up, take up your bed, and walk."
zh: 耶穌對他說：“起來，拿著你的褥子走吧。”

en: And at once the man was healed, and he took up his bed and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
zh: 那人立刻痊愈，就拿起褥子走了。 那天正是安息日。

en: So the Jews said to the man who had been healed, "It is the Sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to take up your bed."
zh: 因此猶太人對那醫好了的人說：“今天是安息日，你拿著褥子是不可以的。”

en: But he answered them, "The man who healed me, that man said to me, 'Take up your bed, and walk.'"
zh: 他卻回答：“那使我痊愈的對我說：‘拿起你的褥子走吧’。”

en: They asked him, "Who is the man who said to you, 'Take up your bed and walk'?"
zh: 他們就問：“那對你說‘拿起來走吧’的是誰？”

en: Now the man who had been healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, as there was a crowd in the place.
zh: 那醫好了的人不知道他是誰，因為那裡人很多，耶穌已經避開了。

en: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, "See, you are well! Sin no more, that nothing worse may happen to you."
zh: 後來，耶穌在殿裡遇見那人，對他說：“你已經痊愈了，不可再犯罪，免得招來更大的禍患。”

en: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had healed him.
zh: 那人就去告訴猶太人，使他痊愈的就是耶穌。

en: And this was why the Jews were persecuting Jesus, because he was doing these things on the Sabbath.
zh: 從此猶太人就迫害耶穌，因為他常常在安息日作這些事。

en: But Jesus answered them, "My Father is working until now, and I am working."
zh: 耶穌卻對他們說：“我父作工直到現在，我也作工。”

en: This was why the Jews were seeking all the more to kill him, because not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.
zh: 因此猶太人就更想殺耶穌，因為他不但破壞安息日，而且稱 神為自己的父，把自己與 神當作平等。

en: So Jesus said to them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of his own accord, but only what he sees the Father doing. For whatever the Father does, that the Son does likewise.
zh: 耶穌又對他們說：“我實實在在告訴你們，子靠著自己不能作甚麼，只能作他看見父所作的；因為父所作的事，子也照樣作。

en: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all that he himself is doing. And greater works than these will he show him, so that you may marvel.
zh: 父愛子，把自己所作的一切指示給他看，還要把比這些更大的事指示給他看，使你們驚奇。

en: For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whom he will.
zh: 父怎樣叫死人復活，使他們得生命，子也照樣隨自己的意思使人得生命。

en: The Father judges no one, but has given all judgment to the Son,
zh: 父不審判人，卻已經把審判的權柄完全交給子，

en: that all may honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
zh: 使所有的人尊敬子好像尊敬父一樣。不尊敬子的，就是不尊敬那差他來的父。

en: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life.
zh: 我實實在在告訴你們，那聽見我的話又信那差我來的，就有永生，不被定罪，而是已經出死入生了。

en: "Truly, truly, I say to you, an hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
zh: 我實實在在告訴你們，時候將到，現在就是了，死人要聽見 神兒子的聲音，聽見的人就要活了。

en: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son also to have life in himself.
zh: 就如父是生命的源頭，照樣他也使子成為生命的源頭，

en: And he has given him authority to execute judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
zh: 並且把執行審判的權柄賜給他，因為他是人子。

en: Do not marvel at this, for an hour is coming when all who are in the tombs will hear his voice
zh: 你們不要把這事看作希奇，因為時候將到，那時所有在墳墓裡的都要聽見他的聲音，

en: and come out, those who have done good to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil to the resurrection of judgment.
zh: 並且都要出來；行善的復活得生命，作惡的復活被定罪。

en: "I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just, because I seek not my own will but the will of him who sent me.
zh: “我靠著自己不能作甚麼，我怎樣聽見，就怎樣審判。我的審判是公義的，因為我不尋求自己的意思，只求那差我來者的旨意。

en: If I alone bear witness about myself, my testimony is not deemed true.
zh: 我若為自己作證，我的見證就不真。

en: There is another who bears witness about me, and I know that the testimony that he bears about me is true.
zh: 然而另有一位為我作證的，我知道他為我作的見證是真的。

en: You sent to John, and he has borne witness to the truth.
zh: 你們曾經派人到約翰那裡，他為真理作過見證。

en: Not that the testimony that I receive is from man, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
zh: 我不接受從人而來的見證，但我說這些事，是要你們得救。

en: He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
zh: 約翰是一盞點亮的燈，你們情願暫時在他的光中歡樂。

en: But the testimony that I have is greater than that of John. For the works that the Father has given me to accomplish, the very works that I am doing, bear witness about me that the Father has sent me.
zh: 但我有比約翰更大的見證，因為父賜給我要我完成的工作，就是我所要作的，證明我是父所差來的。

en: And the Father who sent me has himself borne witness about me. His voice you have never heard, his form you have never seen,
zh: 差我來的父親自為我作了見證。他的聲音，你們從沒有聽過；他的容貌，你們從沒有見過；

en: and you do not have his word abiding in you, for you do not believe the one whom he has sent.
zh: 他的道，你們也不放在心裡，因為你們不信他所差來的那一位。

en: You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me,
zh: 你們研究聖經，因為你們認為聖經中有永生，其實為我作證的就是這聖經，

en: yet you refuse to come to me that you may have life.
zh: 然而你們卻不肯到我這裡來得生命。

en: I do not receive glory from people.
zh: “我不接受從人而來的稱讚；

en: But I know that you do not have the love of God within you.
zh: 我知道你們心裡沒有 神的愛。

en: I have come in my Father's name, and you do not receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.
zh: 我奉我父的名而來，你們不接待我；如果有別人以自己的名義而來，你們倒接待他。

en: How can you believe, when you receive glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the only God?
zh: 你們彼此接受稱讚，卻不尋求從獨一的 神而來的稱讚，怎麼能信呢？

en: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you: Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
zh: 不要以為我要向父控告你們，有一位控告你們的，就是你們所仰賴的摩西。

en: If you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote of me.
zh: 你們若信摩西，也必信我，因為他所寫的書曾論及我。

en: But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe my words?"
zh: 你們若不信他所寫的，怎能信我的話呢？”
